fields.description:
# Tier-1 opportunistic detached drain + `rebar enrich` CLI

## Why
The feature must run async across server/PC/Mac/cloud with ZERO client setup — no daemon, no broker, no scheduler. The proven pattern (git gc --auto, npm update-notifier) piggybacks a cheap, detached drain on ordinary invocations. rebar already ships this exact mechanism for async push (`src/rebar/_store/push.py:108`).

## What
- A `maybe_drain()` gate on ordinary rebar invocations: a cheap check (is anything soaked+unclaimed?) that no-ops in the common case, then DETACHES the enrichment to a child that outlives the command.
- Detach mechanism, cross-platform: POSIX uses `Popen(start_new_session=True, close_fds=True)` (as push.py does); Windows uses `creationflags=DETACHED_PROCESS|CREATE_NO_WINDOW` (start_new_session only maps to CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP on Windows and does NOT orphan-protect — so branch on os.name). Reuse push.py's PYTHONPATH bootstrap so the child can `import rebar`.
- Observability: the detached child does NOT send stderr to DEVNULL — it appends to a rotating log `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log` (git-ignored) so drain failures are diagnosable; only stdout/stdin are DEVNULL.
- The drainer loop: from `pending_enrichment(now)` (S3), claim one (locked read-check-append, S3), run `enrich` (S1), write `TICKET_DIGEST` (S2), append `DONE_ENRICH`; process UP TO `DRAIN_BATCH` tickets per run then exit (bounded — a backlog drains across successive invocations). Idempotent: a re-run overwrites by content-hash.
- `rebar enrich` CLI: `--drain` (bounded drain, the detached target), `--once` (claim+process a single entry, in-process, for tests), `status` (print pending/claimed/soaking counts). Opt-out via the TYPED config `overlap.drain = "off"` (env override `REBAR_OVERLAP_DRAIN`), following the `sync.push` pattern — not a bare ad-hoc env var.

## Dependencies
S1 (enrich op), S2 (digest store), S3 (queue + claim). This story WIRES them; it does not define them. All three are prerequisite sibling stories under this epic, not existing code.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py` (drainer + maybe_drain + detach); CLI arm in `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`; `overlap.drain` in `_config_schema.py`; the opportunistic hook attached to the same post-write path as push (or to `review_plan`/`claim`).
- OUT: native scheduler (idea Tier-2), CI drain (idea Tier-3).

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py`: `--once` processes one entry end-to-end (FakeRunner); `--drain` respects `DRAIN_BATCH` and exits; lock-held → silent skip (exit 0, logged); empty queue → no-op; API-key/[agents] absent → clean no-op; `overlap.drain=off` disables the opportunistic gate; gate latency ~0 when nothing soaked (assert no detach spawned). Windows detach path unit-tested via os.name branch (mock Popen kwargs).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar enrich --drain` processes up to `DRAIN_BATCH` soaked+unclaimed entries then exits; a backlog > DRAIN_BATCH drains across successive runs (AC reconciles "bounded per run", not "all at once"); verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py::test_batch_cap`.
- [ ] An ordinary invocation opportunistically detaches a drain that survives parent exit; when nothing is soaked the gate no-ops with no spawn (assert via test); the foreground command's added latency is within `DRAIN_GATE_BUDGET_MS` (default 20ms).
- [ ] Concurrent drains do not double-process: a second drain finding the lock held skips silently (exit 0) and logs to `.rebar/logs/enrich-drain.log`.
- [ ] With `[agents]`/API key absent, the drain is a clean no-op; `overlap.drain=off` (env `REBAR_OVERLAP_DRAIN`) disables the opportunistic drain entirely.
- [ ] `--once` and `status` are covered: `--once` claims+processes exactly one entry; `status` prints pending/claimed/soaking counts; both have tests.
- [ ] Detach is cross-platform: POSIX via `start_new_session`, Windows via `DETACHED_PROCESS|CREATE_NO_WINDOW`; the child logs errors to a file (never DEVNULL for stderr).
